english: snyán-pa p'yogs bcur grags (snyan pa phyogs bcur grags) every part of the world rings with thy praise; deï snyán-pa rgyán-nas t'os (de'i snyan pa rgyang nas thos) Mil. his praises are heard far and wide; čes deï snyán-pa brjód-čiṅ (ces de'i snyan pa brjod cing) thus speaking praisingly of him Mil.; čes-paï snyán-pa-la rtén-nas (ces pa'i snyan pa la rten nas) owing to a rumour of this purport Mil.; čes snyán-pa daṅ grágs-pa čěn-po byuṅ (ces snyan pa dang grags pa chen po byung) so was said far and near Mil.; deï snyán-du (de'i snyan du) to his praise Mil. (cf. snyan (snyan)). — 2. adj., W. *nyán-po* (*nyan po*), well-sounding, sweet to hear, of voice, words etc.; *t'sor-náṅ-la nyán-po* (*tshor nang la nyan po*) W. pleasant to the ear; also: dge-sloṅ dbyaṅs ráb-tu snyán-pa (dge slong dbyangs rab tu snyan pa) a monk having a well-sounding voice Dzl.; tsig snyán-par (tshig snyan par) with pleasant words Dzl.; snyán-paï tsig-gis (snyan pa'i tshig gis) id.; low, not loud; snyan-skád (snyan skad) also C.: elegant, well-sounding, poetical language; mi snyán-pa (mi snyan pa) 1. unharmonious; 2. offensive, insulting, gaṅ žig bdág-la rtsód-čiṅ mi-snyán-brjod (gang zhig bdag la rtsod cing mi snyan brjod) he who in a dispute says to me insulting words; mi-snyán-par zér-ba dáṅ-du lén-pa (mi snyan par zer ba dang du len pa) to put up with, to pocket offensive remarks. 3. lamentable, skad mi snyán-pa zér-ba (skad mi snyan pa zer ba) to utter lamentable cries, plaintive tones, also of animals, Dzl.; gtam-snyán(-pa) (gtam snyan pa) 1. good, joyful news, glad tidings, byéd-pa (byed pa) to bring them Dzl., Mil. 2. a pleasing talk, conversation Cs. (?) — snyan-grágs (snyan grags) v. grágs-pa (grags pa). — snyan-rgyúd (snyan rgyud) oral instruction of the Lamas, = bka-rgyúd (bka' rgyud). — snyan-(d)ṅág(s) (snyan (d)ngags) v. ṅag (ngag). — 3. vb. to praise, extol, glorify, stód-čiṅ snyán-par grágs-te (stod cing snyan par grags te) he extolled him in songs of praise Dzl. (?)
to smack with the lips Sch.

1. vb. to think, suppose, fancy, imagine, bdág-čag ríṅ-po-če btaṅ (bdag cag ring po che btang) (better: gtaṅ (gtang)) snyám-mo (snyam mo) we think we shall give jewels Dzl. 20V, 16.; ṅa lčeb dgos snyám-nas (nga lceb dgos snyam nas) thinking, I must seek death (v. lčéb-pa (lceb pa)) Pth.; yón-tan daṅ ldán-par snyám-ste (yon tan dang ldan par snyam ste) fancying to be possessed of excellent qualities Dzl. — 2. sbst. thought, sense, mind, feeling, čos byás-na snyám-pa yóṅ-gin gda (chos byas na snyam pa yong gin gda) (cf. na (na) III., 2) we have a mind to renounce the world Mil.; similarly: 'jigs-so snyám-pa yod re-skán ('jigs so snyam pa yod re skan) I am far from any thought of fear Mil.; most frq. snyám-du bsams (snyam du bsams) he thought in his mind; snyam-byéd (snyam byed): p'an snyam-byéd kyaṅ (phan snyam byed kyang) though one may imagine that it will help Med.; skyúg-pa, brduṅs, dkris snyam-byéd (skyug pa, brdungs, dkris snyam byed) there arises a feeling like that of nausea, like that of being beaten, of being (tightly) wrapt up, Med.

1. adj., also snyí-bo (snyi bo), snyí-mo (snyi mo), snyín-po (snyin po) Cs., *nyín-te* (*nyin te*) W., soft, smooth, to the touch; tender, delicate, of the skin; easily broken or injured; loose, crazy, not durable, not strong or stout, of cloth, ropes etc.; not hard or tough, tender, of meat, rendered so by beating or boiling. — 2. sbst. softness. — 3. n. of a plant.
prov. for snyé-ma (snye ma); also Glr.
and snyi-šaṅ-gur-rta (snyi shang gur rta), names of mountains in Nepal.

degenerated, grown worse Cs. snyígs-ma (snyigs ma) 1. impure sediment, már-gyi (mar gyi) in butter; daṅs snyígs byéd-pa (dangs snyigs byed pa) to separate the clear (fluid) from the sediment Med. — 2. the degenerated age (iron age), prop. snyígs-(maï) dus (snyigs ma'i dus).

the heart 1. physically, also snyiṅ-ka (snying ka), snyiṅ-ga (snying ga), resp. t'ugs (-ka) (thugs (-ka)); also the breast; *nyiṅ-ka p'ár-ra rag* (*nying ka phar ra rag*) W. I feel my heart palpitate; snyiṅ 'dár-žiṅ 'gul (snying 'dar zhing 'gul) the heart trembles (with fear) Domaṅ (mdo mang); bdág-gi lús-kyi snyiṅ ltar gčes (bdag gi lus kyi snying ltar gces) as dear to me as my own heart Glr. — 2. intellectually: the mind, snyiṅ dgá-ba (snying dga' ba), snyiṅ bdé-ba (snying bde ba) gladness, cheerfulness; snyiṅ daṅ mig 'p'róg-pa (snying dang mig 'phrog pa) to transport, to ravish Sch.; courage, snyiṅ ma čuṅ čig (snying ma chung cig) be not afraid! sentiment, feeling, will, *nyiṅ sóg-po čó-te ma čag* (*nying sog po co te ma cag*) W. I have not broken it wilfully; *k'a daṅ nyiṅ ma ḍé-te* (*kha dang nying ma de te*) W. hypocritical; *nyiṅ-sém dáṅ-po* (*nying sems dang po*) W. sincere, candid; in a more general sense: snyiṅ gdón-gyis bslus (snying gdon gyis bslus) the heart is infatuated by
